data.title: Solana co-founder Toly: Optimal Capital Formation for Early-Stage Startups May Follow a Three-Step Approach
data.description: Solana co-founder Toly stated that if this model proves effective, he believes the optimal approach for early-stage startups in terms of capital formation likely includes three points: first, providing a staking mechanism for long-term holders; second, releasing more than 20% of the tokens on the TGE day; and third, ideally avoiding the introduction of investors, or if investors are involved, unlocking 100% of the tokens in a single transaction on the same day one year after the TGE. He indicated that the remaining outcomes will depend on Product-Market Fit (PMF).

data.title: Sources: BitGo IPO pricing raised to $18 per share
data.description: Crypto asset custody company BitGo has set its initial public offering (IPO) price at $18 per share, higher than its previous target range of $15 to $17. In this offering, BitGo and some existing shareholders will sell approximately 11.8 million shares. The arrangements are still under discussion, and specific details are subject to change. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Space's prediction market IPO sparks controversy as team retains tens of millions of dollars in oversubscribed funds, citing a "soft cap."
data.description: The public offering of Space, a leveraged prediction market platform within the Solana ecosystem, has sparked controversy. The project, originally planned to raise $2.5 million, actually raised $20 million. The team subsequently announced it would return $7.3 million and retain the remaining oversubscribed funds, arguing that the $2.5 million was a "soft cap" rather than a "hard cap." The project further argued that "$2.5 million can only sustain operations for a few months; this amount represents only the minimum required for initial shipments and is insufficient to support the construction of leveraged prediction market infrastructure over many years."

data.title: Trump Plans Fed Chair Change Amidst Interest Rate Scrutiny
data.description:
Trump plans to appoint a Greenspan-like Fed Chair, impacting monetary policy strategies.
source: https://coincu.com/news/trump-fed-chair-appointment-plans/

data.title: Neynar's Acquisition of Farcaster Sparks Positive Remarks from Mask Founder
data.description:
Mask founder Suji Yan commented on Neynar's acquisition of Farcaster, stating that increased competition is beneficial. According to Odaily, Yan mentioned hearing rumors earlier but did not verify them with the Neynar team. He emphasized the importance of collaboration and congratulated the Neynar team, disclosing his status as a minor shareholder and user of Neynar's software and technology, praising the team's excellence.

In related news, Aave founder Stani announced that the management of the social protocol Lens will be transferred to the Mask team, with the original team serving as technical advisors.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences Dominant Selling Pressure
data.description: The cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a dominant selling pressure, as indicated by the Buy/Sell Pressure Delta metric. According to NS3.AI, this negative reading suggests that sellers are outweighing buyers, which could potentially influence market trends and price movements. Analyst Alphrac highlighted this insight on social media, pointing to the prevailing selling pressure in the market.

data.title: Crypto sectors generally rebounded, with GameFi leading the gains at nearly 7%, and BTC returning above $90,000.
data.description: On January 22nd, according to SoSoValue data, the cryptocurrency market generally rebounded, with the GameFi sector leading the gains at 6.88% in the past 24 hours. Specifically, Axie Infinity (AXS) rose 20.71%, The Sandbox (SAND) rose 13.04%, and Decentraland (MANA) rose 11.03%. Additionally, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 1.16%, surpassing $90,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 1.91%, surpassing $3,000. Meanwhile, MAG7.ssi rose 1.76%, DEFI.ssi rose 1.96%, and MEME.ssi rose 1.79%. In other sectors, the AI ​​sector rose 3.72% in the last 24 hours, with Render (RENDER) up 5.36%; RWA sector up 3.70%, and Pendle (PENDLE) up 11.36%; DeFi sector up 3.14%, with River (RIVER) continuing its upward trend, surging another 36.46%; PayFi sector up 3.12%, with Telcoin (TEL) up 24.98%; Meme sector up 2.47%, with PIPPIN (PIPPIN) up 32.01%; Layer 2 sector up 2.40%, with ImmutableX (IMX) up 6.53%; Layer 1 sector up 1.81%, with Canton Network (CC) up 13.98%; CeFi sector up 1.27%, with Aster (ASTER) up 4.41%. The crypto sector indices, which reflect the historical performance of the sector, show that the ssiGameFi, ssiAI, and ssiRWA indices rose by 9.04%, 4.61%, and 3.90%, respectively.

data.title: U.S. Stock Indices Surge Amid Greenland Tensions Easing
data.description: The major U.S. stock indices experienced a significant rise, with gains expanding to 1%. According to ChainCatcher, this surge follows an announcement by U.S. President Donald Trump, which has notably eased concerns over the situation in Greenland.

data.title: Trump Cancels Greenland Tariff, Boosting U.S. Stocks and Bitcoin
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. U.S. President Donald Trump announced on TruthSocial that the planned Greenland tariff, set to be implemented on February 1, will not proceed. This decision appears to have brought an end to the much-discussed trade tensions between Europe and the United States. Following the announcement, both U.S. stock markets and Bitcoin experienced a rapid rebound, underscoring the correlation between Bitcoin and U.S. stocks, particularly technology stocks.

Currently, U.S. stocks have fully recovered from Tuesday's decline. Although Bitcoin's rebound has not been as strong as that of the stock market, it is approaching $90,000. It is anticipated that Bitcoin could surpass $90,000 during the Asian trading hours tomorrow.

In a personal investment move, I purchased one Bitcoin at $90,000, deviating from my usual strategy of buying without selling. This Bitcoin is intended for short-term trading as an experiment. I am prepared for potential losses, planning to hold if prices drop and considering short-term trades if prices rise. This is a trial approach to my investment strategy.

data.title: Cathie Wood Predicts Bitcoin's Imminent Surge
data.description: BitMine Chair Tom Lee posted on X that Cathie Wood, speaking live on CNBC, has declared that Bitcoin has reached its bottom and is poised for a significant upward movement. Wood's statement suggests optimism about Bitcoin's future trajectory, indicating potential for substantial growth in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: The X platform's "Global Trends" page has added a Meme category section.
data.description: Platform X has updated its "Global Trends" page, adding a new Meme category. This section will showcase various internet memes, funny images, and humorous videos. Users can post and browse currently popular memes and stay informed about mainstream internet slang and trends worldwide.

data.title: The founder of Mask revealed that he is a minor shareholder in Neynar, the company that acquired Farcaster.
data.description: Mask founder Suji Yan commented on Neynar's acquisition of Farcaster in a post on X, saying, "More competition is beneficial. I did hear some rumors earlier, but never confirmed them with the Neynar team. We should strengthen our cooperation, and I sincerely congratulate the Neynar team. Just to disclose, I am a minority shareholder in Neynar, and we are using Neynar's software and technology. They are a very talented group of people." Yesterday, Aave founder Stani stated that management of the social protocol Lens will be transferred to the Mask team, with the original team serving as technical advisors.

data.title: AI Company Humans& Secures $480 Million in Seed Funding
data.description: Humans&, an AI company founded by former members of Anthropic, xAI, and Google, has successfully raised $480 million in seed funding, according to PANews. The company's valuation has reached $4.48 billion, with investors including Nvidia, Jeff Bezos, SV Angel, GV, and Emerson Collective. Humans& aims to develop AI tools that enhance human collaboration, with a team comprising experts from OpenAI, Meta, AI2, and MIT. The company focuses on multi-agent reinforcement learning, AI memory, and user understanding, intending to reshape AI model training and interaction methods.

data.title: Compass Point Urges Caution on Bitcoin Purchases Below $98,000
data.description: Investment bank Compass Point has advised investors to exercise caution when purchasing Bitcoin during price dips until the cryptocurrency surpasses $98,000. According to NS3.AI, this figure represents the average cost basis for short-term holders. Despite Bitcoin's recent rally reaching $97,500, its inability to exceed this threshold has intensified worries about a potential extended downtrend. Additionally, the prevalence of leveraged buying has increased the risk of liquidations, particularly if Bitcoin's price approaches $80,000.

data.title: Seeker (SKR) surged over 280% intraday, pushing its market capitalization above $28 billion.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Seeker (SKR), a native asset of the Solana Mobile ecosystem, saw its intraday price increase by more than 280%, and its market value rose rapidly, once exceeding $28 billion.

data.title: Market Trends Suggest Potential Upswing Before Decline
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X, expressing optimism about market trends, suggesting that historically, peaks often occur before downturns. This perspective is based on past patterns observed in financial markets, where periods of growth precede declines. The statement implies that if historical trends continue, there may be an opportunity for gains before a potential downturn. Investors and analysts often look to historical data to predict future movements, although such predictions are not guaranteed. The sentiment reflects a cautious optimism, encouraging stakeholders to consider historical cycles in their decision-making processes.

data.title: Denmark Rejects Trump's Proposal to Discuss Greenland Purchase
data.description: Denmark's Foreign Minister announced on January 22 that the country has declined U.S. President Donald Trump's request to negotiate the purchase of Greenland. According to BlockBeats, the Danish government has made it clear that Greenland is not for sale, emphasizing its commitment to maintaining the island's current status. The proposal had previously sparked significant international attention and debate.

data.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow Model Forecasts U.S. GDP Growth for Q4 2025
data.description: On January 22, the Atlanta Federal Reserve's GDPNow model has updated its forecast for the United States' GDP growth in the fourth quarter of 2025. According to BlockBeats, the model now predicts a growth rate of 5.4%, slightly higher than the previous estimate of 5.3%. This adjustment reflects ongoing assessments of economic conditions and data influencing the U.S. economy's trajectory.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake announced it will be awarding its employees with Bitcoin, which can only be unlocked after two years of service.
data.description: Steak 'n Shake, the American restaurant chain, announced plans to reward its employees with Bitcoin, but the Bitcoin will not be immediately usable during the vesting period. Employees must work continuously for the company for two years before they can unlock, transfer, or sell the accumulated Bitcoin, a mechanism similar to the vesting period arrangement in stock incentives. Reports indicate that at current Bitcoin price levels, employees working approximately 30 hours per week could receive approximately $327 worth of Bitcoin annually. This reward mechanism is not immediate but implemented through deferred access. This policy comes less than a year after Steak 'n Shake began accepting payments via the Bitcoin Lightning Network last May. The company stated at the time that transaction fees dropped by nearly half within just two weeks of launching Bitcoin payments. In addition to payment scenarios, the company is actively expanding into the cryptocurrency space, including sponsoring the Bitcoin 2025 conference and adding approximately $10 million worth of Bitcoin to its balance sheet to build its "Bitcoin strategic reserve." Steak 'n Shake also launched a Bitcoin-themed meal last October, with each "Bitcoin Meal" donating a small amount to projects supporting Bitcoin's open-source development. Operationally, the company has been continuously adjusting its business in recent years. Its number of US stores has decreased from over 600 in 2018 to approximately 400 by the end of 2025, and it is gradually shifting towards a fast-food model centered on franchise partners and self-service ordering, while retaining the brand's signature retro style.

data.title: Buy/Sell Pressure Delta Indicates Dominant Selling Pressure
data.description: Darkfost posted on X. The Buy/Sell Pressure Delta remains negative, suggesting that selling pressure is currently dominant in the market. This metric, highlighted by Alphrac, provides insight into the ongoing market trends and investor behavior. The negative delta indicates that sellers are outweighing buyers, which could impact market dynamics and pricing strategies.
